prompt:
Add episode-level monitoring tools to Sonarr. The codebase was recently refactored — tools and dispatch are split into per-service files. You MUST use the new structure.

## File structure (IMPORTANT — use these files, not tools.go or agent.go)
- Tool definitions go in: internal/agent/tools_sonarr.go (has sonarrToolDefs() and sonarr input structs)
- Dispatch logic goes in: internal/agent/dispatch_sonarr.go (has dispatchSonarr method)
- Client methods go in: internal/sonarr/client.go
- Types go in: internal/sonarr/types.go
- Destructive tools map is in: internal/agent/tools.go (the only thing that stays in the shared file)

## Sonarr API endpoints

### 1. Bulk update episode monitoring
PUT /api/v3/episode/monitor
Body: {"episodeIds": [1, 2, 3], "monitored": true}

### 2. Update single episode
PUT /api/v3/episode/{id}
Fetch episode, set monitored, PUT back.

## Implementation

### A. internal/sonarr/client.go — add to Client interface + implement:
- MonitorEpisodes(ctx context.Context, episodeIDs []int, monitored bool) error — PUT /api/v3/episode/monitor
- UpdateEpisode(ctx context.Context, episode *Episode) (*Episode, error) — PUT /api/v3/episode/{id}

### B. internal/sonarr/types.go — add:
type MonitorEpisodesRequest struct {
    EpisodeIDs []int `json:"episodeIds"`
    Monitored  bool  `json:"monitored"`
}

### C. internal/agent/tools_sonarr.go — add input structs + tool definitions:
1. update_episode_monitoring — input: episodeID int, monitored bool. Mark destructive.
2. monitor_season_episodes — input: seriesID int, seasonNumber int, monitored bool. Mark destructive. Bulk-monitors all episodes in a season.

### D. internal/agent/dispatch_sonarr.go — add cases:
1. update_episode_monitoring: call a.sonarr.MonitorEpisodes with single episode ID
2. monitor_season_episodes: call a.sonarr.GetEpisodes, filter by seasonNumber, call MonitorEpisodes with all matching IDs

### E. internal/agent/tools.go — add to destructiveTools map:
"update_episode_monitoring": true,
"monitor_season_episodes": true,

## Testing
Run 'go test ./...' before creating the PR.
